judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports fully hit all three expectations with comparable depth and accuracy; Report 2 additionally proposes FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY as defense-in-depth alongside the role split, which is extra but not contrary to the expected fix.

report_1.evidence: In Postgres, RLS policies are silently skipped for both superusers and table owners (unless FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY is set, which this migration does not do). So current_setting('app.current_tenant') is being set correctly, but the policy that would filter on it is never even evaluated for this role — GET /clients runs as an effectively-unrestricted SELECT and returns rows from every tenant

report_2.evidence: In Postgres, table owners (and superusers) bypass row-level security policies by default, unless the table has FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY set — which 0001_init.sql never does. So even though SET app.current_tenant = ... runs correctly and the tenant_isolation policy exists, it's never evaluated for this connection: cedar_owner sails straight through RLS

report_1.evidence: Structurally, going forward the app should never have a single ambiguous DATABASE_URL that could point at either role. Two distinct connection strings should exist: - One for schema-owning operations only (cedar_owner), used exclusively by scripts/migrate.sh, scripts/reset-local-db.sh, and db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql. ... - One for everything the running FastAPI app does (cedar_app), consumed by backend/app/db.py.

report_2.evidence: Structurally: keep cedar_owner privileged for schema migrations only (DDL), and make cedar_app — the least-privileged, RLS-bound role — the only role the running application ever connects with.

report_1.evidence: cedar_app is created only by the one-time, hand-run script db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql ... cedarline/scripts/reset-local-db.sh does: docker compose down -v ... docker compose up -d db ... ./scripts/migrate.sh ... It never re-invokes db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql. So after a wipe+rebuild, cedar_app simply doesn't exist in the new cluster.

report_2.evidence: docker compose down -v wipes cedar_pgdata, so on the next up, Postgres reinitializes from scratch and only recreates the single bootstrap role from POSTGRES_USER (cedar_owner). The cedar_app role was only ever created by the separate, manual, undocumented-in-scripts step ... reset-local-db.sh ... never calls this bootstrap script — it only calls migrate.sh
